dialogos.texto_en: That was fun, right?\nI didn't know it was such a bustling place.
I feel like I saw a lifetime's worth\nof Okinawan souvenirs.
But in a way, it's scary that we still haven't seen\nall the shops.
Including the backstreets, I wonder how many there are.
If we keep looking around absentmindedly,\nthe day will end before we know it.
Now, onto the main topic.\nAnyone find anything that looks like a good souvenir?
Nope.
When I start thinking about it, I'm getting overwhelmed.
I had a few things catch my eye,\nbut I haven't found the perfect thing yet.
It's an important decision,\nso I don't want to just choose something randomly.
Yeah, it's a once-in-a-lifetime memory, after all.
You're right...
(Actually, I wish I could have suggested something\n to everyone, if I understood what they are going for earlier.)
Well, well, we've only just started looking, right?\nAnd our next stop is American Village.
What's that like?
As the name suggests, it's a resort with townscapes that looks\nlike an American village.
It's also a great shopping spot,\nI think it's perfect for looking for souvenirs.
Also, the view is great. It's right by the sea.\nThere's even a Ferris wheel.
Can you see the ocean from the Ferris wheel?\nI want to ride it!
Let's ride it guys!\nLet's ride it as many times as we want!
You can ride it as many times as you want.\nI won't stop you, but let's go in moderation.
Alright then, let's go.
Yeah.
